title: Order 2025/3 of 30 October 2025
text:
List of Cases No. 33 Order 2025/3
INTERNATIONAL TRIBUNAL FOR THE LAW OF THE SEA
YEAR 2025
30 October 2025
THE "ZHENG HE" CASE
(LUXEMBOURG v. MEXICO)
ORDER
The President of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea,
Having regard to article 27 of the Statute of the Tribunal,
Having regard to article 59 of the Rules of the Tribunal,
Having regard to the Orders of the President of the Tribunal of 8 August 2024
and 3 February 2025,
Makes the following Order:
1. Whereas, pursuant to the Order of 8 August 2024, the President of the
Tribunal fixed 10 February 2025 and 11 August 2025 as the time limits for the filing,
respectively, of the Memorial of Luxembourg and the Counter-Memorial of Mexico;
2. Whereas, pursuant to the Order of 3 February 2025, the President extended
to 24 March 2025 and 3 November 2025 the time limits for the filing, respectively, of

List of Cases No. 33 2 Order 2025/3
the Memorial of Luxembourg and the Counter-Memorial of Mexico; and whereas the
Memorial of Luxembourg was filed within the time limit thus extended;
3. Whereas, by a joint letter received by the Registry of the Tribunal on
30 October 2025, the Agent of Luxembourg and the Agent of Mexico stated that "the
Parties have agreed to respectfully request the Tribunal for a six-week extension" of
the time limit fixed for the submission of the Counter-Memorial of Mexico to
15 December 2025;
4. Whereas, in the same letter, the Agents of the Parties informed the Tribunal
that, "following constructive bilateral exchanges, they have formalized a Letter of
Understanding through exchange of notes, in order to achieve a complete and
definitive solution of the situation of the vessel Zheng He";
5. Being satisfied that there is adequate justification for the request for an
extension of the time limit;
THE PRESIDENT
Taking into account the agreement of the Parties,
Extends the time limit for the filing of the Counter-Memorial of Mexico to
15 December 2025;
Reserves the subsequent procedure for further decision.
Done in English and in French, both texts being equally authoritative, in the
Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg, this thirtieth day of October, two thousand and
twenty-five, in three copies, one of which will be placed in the archives of the

List of Cases No. 33 3 Order 2025/3
Tribunal and the others transmitted to the Government of Luxembourg and to the
Government of Mexico, respectively.
